serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of rehydrated bean curd sticks, often used in stir-fries or soups, is about 100 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Tofu skin, dried (Food ID: 00012)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are for dried tofu skin, which is the base ingredient for bean curd sticks. The food is typically rehydrated and cooked before consumption, which would alter the per 100g values of the final prepared dish.

gi_source.title: Tofu, firm (analogous food)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index estimated based on firm tofu, as bean curd sticks (dried tofu skin) have a similar low carbohydrate profile and are derived from soybeans. Direct GI for bean curd sticks not found.

hypertension.guidance: Bean curd sticks are naturally low in sodium, making them a suitable food choice for individuals managing hypertension, provided they are prepared without excessive salt.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and high protein content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a low glycemic index and high protein content, bean curd sticks can help manage blood sugar levels and promote satiety, making them beneficial for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low in saturated fat and cholesterol-free
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Bean curd sticks are low in saturated fat and contain no cholesterol, contributing to a heart-healthy diet and supporting cardiovascular health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bean Curd Sticks, also known as Fuzhu or dried tofu skin, are a traditional East Asian food product made from the film that forms on the surface of boiled soy milk. As the film is lifted and dried, it forms sheets or sticks with a unique chewy texture. They are typically rehydrated before use and can be braised, fried, added to soups, stews, or hot pots, absorbing the flavors of the surrounding ingredients. Nutritionally, bean curd sticks are a rich source of plant-based protein and are naturally low in carbohydrates and cholesterol-free. They have a low glycemic index, making them a beneficial food for blood sugar management and overall heart health.

confidence_reason: Nutrient data for dried tofu skin (bean curd sticks) is an exact match from HPB Singapore Foodo. Glycemic index is an analogous estimate based on firm tofu from the University of Sydney GI Database due to similar nutritional profile and lack of direct data.
notes: Nutrient values are for dried tofu skin (bean curd sticks). When rehydrated and cooked, the per 100g values of the prepared dish will differ due to water absorption and added ingredients. `potassium_mg`, `vitamin_c_mg`, `vitamin_a_mcg_rae`, `vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg`, `vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg`, `vitamin_b3_niacin_mg`, `vitamin_b6_mg`, `vitamin_b12_mcg`, `folate_mcg_dfe`, `vitamin_d_mcg`, `vitamin_e_mg`, `phosphorus_mg`, `magnesium_mg`, `zinc_mg`, `selenium_mcg` are -1 as they are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo source for this item. `sugar_g` and `cholesterol_mg` are confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo.
